title: Git-first mindset
summary: Version control as the backbone of all work

sightseeings.title: Commits & PRs
sightseeings.summary: Atomic commits, meaningful messages, pull request etiquette
sightseeings.details:
A git-first mindset starts with understanding commits as snapshots of intent, not just saves.

• Write commits that tell a story — each one should answer "why"
• Open pull requests as a communication tool, not just a merge mechanism
• Review PRs as collaborative quality gates, not gatekeeping
• Use draft PRs to share work-in-progress and gather early feedback

sightseeings.title: Cloning & Forking
sightseeings.summary: Working with repositories locally and contributing upstream
sightseeings.details:
Understanding the difference between cloning and forking unlocks how open-source and inner-source collaboration works.

• Clone: get a full local copy of a repo you have direct access to
• Fork: create your own copy of someone else's repo to propose changes
• Set upstream remotes to stay in sync with the origin
• Know when to branch vs. when to fork

sightseeings.title: Merge Strategies
sightseeings.summary: Merge commits, squash, rebase — choosing the right strategy
sightseeings.details:
How you integrate changes shapes the history and the team's ability to understand evolution.

• Merge commit: preserves full branch history, clear but can be noisy
• Squash merge: collapses a branch into one clean commit on main
• Rebase: rewrites commits on top of main — linear history, requires discipline
• Understand fast-forward vs. three-way merges
• Team conventions matter more than the "best" strategy

sightseeings.title: Task expansion
sightseeings.summary: We should probably discuss all these.
sightseeings.details:
- from low code tools to git first way of working
- from reporting to fixing
- from reviewing requirements through transforming to test cases to active contributions in context engineering
- from functional to visual and parafunctional
- from trustworthy specs to generated specs with need of alignment
- from separating test design and automation to combining them into continuous test automation
- from verifying against specs to verifying against alignment for resultsful testing (seeing problems that needs seeing)
- from individual contributor flows to team collaboration

sightseeings.title: Token optimization and FinOps
sightseeings.summary: Seeing what AI costs for value generation

sightseeings.title: Intent lead
sightseeings.summary: Decides what must be learned, protected, proven, or observed.
sightseeings.details:
- Define quality outcomes, not just features
- Align acceptance criteria with measurable business value
- Prioritize risks: regulatory, security, reputation, usability, AI hallucination
- Define “done” beyond functionality: reliable, explainable, observable, resilient
- Drive production quality KPIs: defect escape rate, customer friction, AI response accuracy, trust score, SLA/SLO adherence

sightseeings.title: Value engineer
sightseeings.summary: Continuously experiments to discover whether the system creates value safely and effectively.
sightseeings.details:
- Create continuous evaluation frameworks
- Validate: correctness, usefulness, consistency, personalization quality
- Run: A/B experiments, telemetry analysis. behavior-driven evaluations
- Establish “golden datasets”
- Monitor production drift: model drift. response variance, performance degradation
- Quantify business impact: reduced effort, accuracy improvements, faster decisions

sightseeings.title: Context engineer
sightseeings.summary: Creates the shared understanding that makes meaningful testing possible.
sightseeings.details:
- Engineer high-quality prompts and grounding data
- Create testable AI contexts
- Validate: prompt consistency, retrieval quality, context relevance, memory/window limitations
- Design synthetic and edge-case datasets
- Prevent: context poisoning, prompt injection, stale knowledge
- Build traceability between: requirements, prompts, outputs, evaluations

sightseeings.title: Alignment engineer
sightseeings.summary: Ensures the system stays coherent, trustworthy, and directionally correct.
sightseeings.details:
- Verify AI behavior aligns with: policies, regulations, ethical constraints, brand standards
- Conduct adversarial testing: jailbreak attempts, prompt injection, malicious inputs
- Validate: fairness, explainability, auditability, transparency
- Define trust controls: human-in-the-loop, approval workflows, risk thresholds
- Ensure AI observability and traceability

sightseeings.title: Experience engineer
sightseeings.summary: Makes system quality observable, understandable, and actionable for humans.
sightseeings.details:
- Validate conversational quality
- Ensure: clarity, cognitive simplicity, accessibility, responsiveness
- Test multi-turn interactions
- Validate confidence indicators and fallback behavior
- Assess human trust and adoption
- Ensure graceful degradation when AI confidence is low
